2606.31781.title: SpikeLogBERT: Energy-Efficient Log Parsing Using Spiking Transformer Networks
2606.31781.paper_summary: Log parsing is a fundamental step in automated log analysis, transforming raw system logs into structured event templates for downstream tasks such as anomaly detection and system monitoring. Existing log parsing methods range from rule-based and clustering-based approaches to neural models that learn semantic representations from log messages. However, neural approaches typically rely on dense matrix multiplications, which can result in high computational cost and energy consumption. This paper presents SpikeLogBERT, a spiking neural network framework for energy-efficient log parsing. The proposed model integrates a spiking transformer architecture with knowledge distillation from a BERT teacher model, enabling spike-driven computation while preserving semantic representation capability. By leveraging sparse spike activations and event-driven processing, the number of active operations during inference can be significantly reduced. As an initial benchmark study, experiments on the HDFS dataset demonstrate that SpikeLogBERT outperforms ANN-based neural log parsing models with a parsing accuracy of 0.99997, while reducing estimated theoretical energy consumption by up to 62.6% under standard 45nm CMOS assumptions.
2606.31781.paper_summary_zh: 日誌解析是自動化日誌分析中的基本步驟，將原始系統日誌轉換為結構化事件模板，以便於後續任務，如異常檢測和系統監控。現有的日誌解析方法範圍從基於規則和基於聚類的方法到從日誌消息中學習語義表示的神經模型。然而，神經方法通常依賴於密集的矩陣乘法，這可能導致高計算成本和能量消耗。本文提出了SpikeLogBERT，一種用於能效日誌解析的脈衝神經網絡框架。所提出的模型將脈衝Transformer架構與來自BERT教師模型的知識蒸餾相結合，實現了驅動脈衝的計算，同時保留語義表示能力。通過利用稀疏脈衝激活和事件驅動處理，推理過程中的活躍操作數量可以顯著減少。作為初步基準研究，對HDFS數據集的實驗表明，SpikeLogBERT在解析準確率達到0.99997的同時，超越了基於ANN的神經日誌解析模型，並在標準45nm CMOS假設下將估計的理論能量消耗降低了高達62.6%。
2606.31781.author: Thuan Bui et.al.
2606.31781.authors: Thuan Bui, Duong Do, Tung Vu, Duc-Tho Mai, Cong-Kha Pham
